search.h(3) Library Functions Manual search.h(3)

NAME

search.h - Interactive search filtering for terminal screens.

SYNOPSIS

Enumerations


enum log_search_mode_t { LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ACTIVE }
Search mode states.

Functions


asciichat_error_t log_search_init (void)
Initialize interactive grep subsystem. void log_search_destroy (void)
Clean up interactive grep subsystem. void log_search_enter_mode (void)
Enter search mode (user pressed '/'). void log_search_exit_mode (bool accept)
Exit search mode. bool log_search_is_entering (void)
Check if currently in input mode (typing pattern). bool log_search_is_active (void)
Check if filtering is active. bool log_search_should_handle (int key)
Check if a key should be handled by grep module. asciichat_error_t log_search_handle_key (keyboard_key_t key)
Process keyboard input for grep. asciichat_error_t log_search_gather_and_filter_logs (session_log_entry_t **out_entries, size_t *out_count)
Gather and filter logs for display. void log_search_render_input_line (int width)
Render grep input line at bottom of screen. bool log_search_get_match_info (const char *message, size_t *out_match_start, size_t *out_match_len)
Get match info for highlighting a log message. bool log_search_get_global_highlight (void)
Check if global highlighting (/g flag) is enabled in current pattern. void * log_search_get_pattern_singleton (void)
Get the compiled regex pattern for interactive grep (internal use). bool log_search_is_entering_atomic (void)
Check if grep is in entering mode (async-signal-safe). void log_search_signal_cancel (void)
Cancel grep mode from a signal handler (async-signal-safe). bool log_search_check_signal_cancel (void)
Check and clear the signal-cancel flag. bool log_search_needs_rerender (void)
Check if screen needs immediate re-render. void * log_search_get_mutex (void)
Get the mutex protecting grep state. int log_search_get_input_len (void)
Get current input buffer length (must hold mutex). int log_search_get_cursor_position (void)
Get current cursor position in input buffer (must hold mutex). const char * log_search_get_input_buffer (void)
Get current input buffer content (must hold mutex). bool log_search_get_case_insensitive (void)
Check if case-insensitive flag is set (must hold mutex).

Detailed Description

Interactive search filtering for terminal screens.

Provides vim-style / search functionality for status and splash screens. Users can press / to activate search mode, type a pattern with full /pattern/flags syntax support, and see logs filter in real-time.

Features:

  • Press / to enter search mode
  • Bottom line becomes search input: /<pattern>█ or /<pattern>/flags█
  • Supports full flag syntax: i, F, g, I, A<n>, B<n>, C<n>
  • Live filtering as user types
  • Enter accepts, Escape/Ctrl+C cancels
  • Integrates with both status screen and splash screen

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file search.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum log_search_mode_t

Search mode states.

Enumerator

LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE
Not in search mode.
LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING
LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ACTIVE
Pattern accepted and filtering.

35   LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ACTIVE

  Documentation




bool log_search_check_signal_cancel (void )


Check and clear the signal-cancel flag.


Returns


true if grep was cancelled by a signal since last
  check


Called by the status screen loop to detect signal-initiated
    cancellation.

381   bool expected = true;
382   return atomic_cas_bool(&g_search_state.signal_cancelled, &expected, false);

void
  log_search_destroy (void )


Clean up interactive grep subsystem. Frees all allocated
    resources. Safe to call multiple times.


Definition at line 192 of file search.c.


192                               {
193   if (!lifecycle_shutdown(&g_log_search_lifecycle)) {
194     return; // Already shut down or never initialized
195   }
196 
197   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
198 
199   // Free active patterns
200   for (int i = 0; i < g_search_state.active_pattern_count; i++) {
201     if (g_search_state.active_patterns[i]) {
202       // Note: pcre2_singleton handles its own reference counting
203       g_search_state.active_patterns[i] = NULL;
204     }
205   }
206 
207   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
208 }






References lifecycle_shutdown(), mutex_lock, and
    mutex_unlock.






void
  log_search_enter_mode (void )


Enter search mode (user pressed '/'). Saves current CLI --grep
    patterns, activates input mode, and prepares for interactive pattern
  entry.


Definition at line 214 of file search.c.


214                                  {
215   // Ensure cursor is visible for user typing
216   (void)terminal_cursor_show();
217 
218   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
219 
220   // Save current patterns (CLI --grep patterns)
221   asciichat_error_t result = grep_save_patterns();
222   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
223     log_warn("Failed to save filter patterns");
224   }
225 
226   // Clear input buffer
227   memset(g_search_state.input_buffer, 0, sizeof(g_search_state.input_buffer));
228   g_search_state.len = 0;
229   g_search_state.cursor = 0;
230 
231   // Pre-populate with CLI --grep pattern if available (only once at startup)
232   if (!g_search_state.cli_pattern_auto_populated) {
233     const char *cli_pattern = grep_get_last_pattern();
234     if (cli_pattern && cli_pattern[0] != '\0') {
235       // Skip leading slash if present (prompt already shows "/")
236       const char *pattern_to_use = cli_pattern;
237       if (cli_pattern[0] == '/') {
238         pattern_to_use = cli_pattern + 1;
239       }
240 
241       size_t pattern_len = strlen(pattern_to_use);
242       if (pattern_len < sizeof(g_search_state.input_buffer) - 1) {
243         memcpy(g_search_state.input_buffer, pattern_to_use, pattern_len + 1);
244         g_search_state.len = pattern_len;
245         g_search_state.cursor = pattern_len;
246         g_search_state.cli_pattern_auto_populated = true;
247 
248         // Compile and apply the pattern immediately so it starts filtering
249         grep_parse_result_t parsed = grep_parse_pattern(g_search_state.input_buffer);
250         if (parsed.valid) {
251           // Store parsed flags
252           g_search_state.case_insensitive = parsed.case_insensitive;
253           g_search_state.fixed_string = parsed.is_fixed_string;
254           g_search_state.global_highlight = parsed.global_flag;
255           g_search_state.invert_match = parsed.invert;
256           g_search_state.context_before = parsed.context_before;
257           g_search_state.context_after = parsed.context_after;
258 
259           // Compile pattern if not fixed string
260           if (strlen(parsed.pattern) > 0 && !parsed.is_fixed_string) {
261             pcre2_singleton_t *singleton = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(parsed.pattern, parsed.pcre2_options);
262             if (singleton) {
263               pcre2_code *code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(singleton);
264               if (code) {
265                 g_search_state.active_patterns[0] = singleton;
266                 g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 1;
267               } else {
268                 g_search_state.fixed_string = true;
269               }
270             } else {
271               g_search_state.fixed_string = true;
272             }
273           }
274         }
275       }
276     }
277   }
278 
279   // Enter input mode
280   g_search_state.mode = LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING;
281   atomic_store_int(&g_search_state.mode_atomic, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING);
282   atomic_store_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender, true);
283 
284   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
285 }

    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(),
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(), atomic_store_bool(),
    atomic_store_int(), grep_parse_result_t::case_insensitive,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_after,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_before,
    grep_parse_result_t::global_flag, grep_get_last_pattern(),
    grep_parse_pattern(), grep_save_patterns(),
    grep_parse_result_t::invert,
    grep_parse_result_t::is_fixed_string,
    LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING, log_warn, mutex_lock,
    mutex_unlock, grep_parse_result_t::pattern,

    and grep_parse_result_t::valid.


Referenced by log_search_handle_key(),
    ui_status_display(), and ui_status_display_interactive().






void
  log_search_exit_mode (bool accept)


Exit search mode.


Parameters


accept If true, compile and activate typed
  pattern; if false, restore previous patterns


If accepting: Parses pattern with /pattern/flags syntax, compiles
    with PCRE2, and activates filtering. If canceling: Restores CLI --grep
    patterns.


Definition at line 287 of file search.c.


287                                        {
288   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
289 
290   if (g_search_state.mode != LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING) {
291     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
292     return;
293   }
294 
295   if (!accept) {
296     // Cancel - restore previous patterns and reset CLI pattern flag so user can re-populate
297     asciichat_error_t result = grep_restore_patterns();
298     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
299       log_warn("Failed to restore filter patterns");
300     }
301 
302     // Clear interactive grep patterns
303     for (int i = 0; i < g_search_state.active_pattern_count; i++) {
304       g_search_state.active_patterns[i] = NULL;
305     }
306     g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 0;
307 
308     // Reset the CLI pattern auto-populated flag so user can re-enter grep mode with the pattern again
309     g_search_state.cli_pattern_auto_populated = false;
310 
311     g_search_state.mode = LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE;
312     atomic_store_int(&g_search_state.mode_atomic, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE);
313     atomic_store_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender, true);
314     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
315     return;
316   }
317 
318   // Accept - parse and compile pattern
319   grep_parse_result_t parsed = grep_parse_pattern(g_search_state.input_buffer);
320 
321   if (!parsed.valid) {
322     log_error("Invalid pattern format");
323     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
324     return; // Stay in input mode
325   }
326 
327   // Clear old regex patterns
328   for (int i = 0; i < g_search_state.active_pattern_count; i++) {
329     g_search_state.active_patterns[i] = NULL;
330   }
331   g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 0;
332 
333   // Store parsed flags first
334   g_search_state.case_insensitive = parsed.case_insensitive;
335   g_search_state.fixed_string = parsed.is_fixed_string;
336   g_search_state.global_highlight = parsed.global_flag;
337   g_search_state.invert_match = parsed.invert;
338   g_search_state.context_before = parsed.context_before;
339   g_search_state.context_after = parsed.context_after;
340 
341   // Compile and store new pattern (if not fixed string)
342   if (strlen(parsed.pattern) > 0 && !parsed.is_fixed_string) {
343     pcre2_singleton_t *singleton = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(parsed.pattern, parsed.pcre2_options);
344     if (singleton) {
345       // Verify the pattern actually compiles
346       pcre2_code *code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(singleton);
347       if (code) {
348         g_search_state.active_patterns[0] = singleton;
349         g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 1;
350       } else {
351         // Compilation failed - fall back to fixed string matching
352         g_search_state.fixed_string = true; // Override to use fixed string instead
353       }
354     } else {
355       // Malloc failed - fall back to fixed string
356       g_search_state.fixed_string = true;
357     }
358   }
359   // For fixed strings, pattern stays in input_buffer and fixed_string=true
360 
361   g_search_state.mode = LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ACTIVE;
362   atomic_store_int(&g_search_state.mode_atomic, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ACTIVE);
363   atomic_store_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender, true);
364 
365   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
366 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(),
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(), atomic_store_bool(),
    atomic_store_int(), grep_parse_result_t::case_insensitive,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_after,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_before,
    grep_parse_result_t::global_flag, grep_parse_pattern(),
    grep_restore_patterns(), grep_parse_result_t::invert,
    grep_parse_result_t::is_fixed_string, log_error,
    LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ACTIVE, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING,
    LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE, log_warn, mutex_lock,
    mutex_unlock, grep_parse_result_t::pattern,
    grep_parse_result_t::pcre2_options, and
    grep_parse_result_t::valid.


Referenced by log_search_handle_key(), and
    ui_status_display_interactive().






asciichat_error_t log_search_gather_and_filter_logs
  (session_log_entry_t ** out_entries, size_t * out_count)


Gather and filter logs for display.


Parameters


out_entries Output array of filtered log entries
  (caller must free with SAFE_FREE)


out_count Output count of filtered entries


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Hybrid approach:



  
1.

  
If --log-file specified: tail last N KB, parse entries

  
2.

  
Fetch entries from in-memory session_log_buffer (up to 100
    entries)

  
3.

  
Merge and deduplicate by timestamp

  
4.

  
Filter combined set with active PCRE2 patterns

  
5.

  
Return filtered entries (caller must free)




If no log file or read error: Falls back to in-memory buffer
  only.


Definition at line 530 of file search.c.


530                                                                                                           {
531   if (!out_entries || !out_count) {
532     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "out_entries and out_count must not be NULL");
533   }
534 
535   // Get logs from in-memory buffer
536   session_log_entry_t *buffer_entries =
537       SAFE_MALLOC(SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE * sizeof(session_log_entry_t), session_log_entry_t *);
538   if (!buffer_entries) {
539     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate log buffer");
540   }
541 
542   session_log_buffer_t *log_buf = log_get_session_log_buffer();
543   size_t buffer_count = session_log_buffer_get_recent(log_buf, buffer_entries, SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE);
544 
545   // Check if filtering is active (either regex patterns or fixed string)
546   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
547   bool has_regex_patterns = (g_search_state.active_pattern_count > 0);
548   bool has_fixed_string = (g_search_state.fixed_string && g_search_state.len > 0);
549   bool filtering_active = has_regex_patterns || has_fixed_string;
550 
551   if (!filtering_active) {
552     // No filtering active - return all entries
553     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
554     *out_entries = buffer_entries;
555     *out_count = buffer_count;
556     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
557   }
558 
559   // Filter entries with PCRE2
560   session_log_entry_t *filtered =
561       SAFE_MALLOC(SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE * sizeof(session_log_entry_t), session_log_entry_t *);
562   if (!filtered) {
563     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
564     SAFE_FREE(buffer_entries);
565     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate filtered buffer");
566   }
567 
568   size_t filtered_count = 0;
569 
570   // Create match data for PCRE2 matching
571   pcre2_match_data *match_data = NULL;
572   for (int p = 0; p < g_search_state.active_pattern_count; p++) {
573     if (g_search_state.active_patterns[p]) {
574       pcre2_code *code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(g_search_state.active_patterns[p]);
575       if (code) {
576         match_data = pcre2_match_data_create_from_pattern(code, NULL);
577         break;
578       }
579     }
580   }
581 
582   if (!match_data && g_search_state.active_pattern_count > 0) {
583     // Couldn't create match data - fall back to returning all entries
584     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
585     SAFE_FREE(filtered);
586     *out_entries = buffer_entries;
587     *out_count = buffer_count;
588     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
589   }
590 
591   // Parse pattern once before loop (for fixed string mode)
592   grep_parse_result_t parsed = {0};
593   if (has_fixed_string) {
594     parsed = grep_parse_pattern(g_search_state.input_buffer);
595   }
596 
597   for (size_t i = 0; i < buffer_count; i++) {
598     const char *original_message = buffer_entries[i].message;
599     bool matches = false;
600 
601     // Strip ANSI codes to match against plain text (consistent with highlighting)
602     char plain_message[SESSION_LOG_LINE_MAX] = {0};
603     const char *strip_ansi_codes_fn(const char *src, char *dst, size_t dst_size);
604     // Use inline stripping to avoid function call overhead
605     {
606       size_t pos = 0;
607       const char *src = original_message;
608       char *dst = plain_message;
609       size_t dst_size = sizeof(plain_message);
610 
611       while (*src && pos < dst_size - 1) {
612         if (*src == '\x1b' && src[1] != '\0' && src[1] == '[') {
613           // CSI sequence - skip until final byte
614           src += 2;
615           while (*src && pos < dst_size - 1 && !(*src >= 0x40 && *src <= 0x7E)) {
616             src++;
617           }
618           if (*src)
619             src++; // Skip final byte
620         } else {
621           dst[pos++] = *src++;
622         }
623       }
624       dst[pos] = '\0';
625     }
626 
627     size_t message_len = strlen(plain_message);
628 
629     // Fixed string matching
630     if (has_fixed_string) {
631       const char *search_pattern = parsed.pattern;
632 
633       if (g_search_state.case_insensitive) {
634         // Unicode-aware case-insensitive fixed string search
635         matches = (utf8_strcasestr(plain_message, search_pattern) != NULL);
636       } else {
637         // Case-sensitive fixed string search
638         matches = (strstr(plain_message, search_pattern) != NULL);
639       }
640     }
641     // Regex pattern matching
642     else if (has_regex_patterns) {
643       // Check against all active patterns (OR logic)
644       for (int p = 0; p < g_search_state.active_pattern_count; p++) {
645         pcre2_singleton_t *singleton = g_search_state.active_patterns[p];
646         if (!singleton) {
647           continue;
648         }
649 
650         pcre2_code *code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(singleton);
651         if (!code) {
652           continue;
653         }
654 
655         // Match against plain message (without ANSI codes)
656         int rc = pcre2_jit_match(code, (PCRE2_SPTR)plain_message, message_len, 0, 0, match_data, NULL);
657         if (rc >= 0) {
658           matches = true;
659           break;
660         }
661       }
662     }
663 
664     // Apply invert logic
665     if (g_search_state.invert_match) {
666       matches = !matches;
667     }
668 
669     if (matches) {
670       filtered[filtered_count++] = buffer_entries[i];
671     }
672   }
673 
674   // Free match data
675   if (match_data) {
676     pcre2_match_data_free(match_data);
677   }
678 
679   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
680 
681   SAFE_FREE(buffer_entries);
682   *out_entries = filtered;
683   *out_count = filtered_count;
684   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
685 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(), ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_MEMORY, grep_parse_pattern(),
    log_get_session_log_buffer(), session_log_entry_t::message,
    mutex_lock, mutex_unlock, grep_parse_result_t::pattern,
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    session_log_buffer_get_recent(), SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE,
    SESSION_LOG_LINE_MAX, SET_ERRNO, and
  utf8_strcasestr().

bool log_search_get_global_highlight (void )


Check if global highlighting (/g flag) is enabled in current
    pattern.


Returns


true if interactive grep is active and /g flag is set,
  false otherwise


Used by filter highlighting code to determine if all matches
    should be highlighted or just the first match. Returns false when
    interactive grep is inactive.


Definition at line 895 of file search.c.


895                                            {
896   // Use atomic read to avoid mutex contention with keyboard handler
897   int mode = atomic_load_int(&g_search_state.mode_atomic);
898   if (mode == LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE) {
899     return false;
900   }
901 
902   // Safe read under mutex (quick operation)
903   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);

905   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);

bool log_search_get_match_info (const char * message,
  size_t * out_match_start, size_t * out_match_len)


Get match info for highlighting a log message.


Parameters


message Log message to check


out_match_start Output: start position of match in plain text


out_match_len Output: length of match


Returns


true if message matches current filter pattern, false
  otherwise


Used by display code to apply highlighting to matching portions of
    logs. Returns match position for use with log_filter_highlight().


Definition at line 728 of file search.c.


728                                                                                                     {
729   if (!message || !out_match_start || !out_match_len) {
730     return false;
731   }
732 
733   *out_match_start = 0;
734   *out_match_len = 0;
735 
736   // Use atomic read to avoid mutex contention with keyboard handler
737   int mode = atomic_load_int(&g_search_state.mode_atomic);
738   if (mode == LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE) {
739     return false;
740   }
741 
742   // Make a safe copy of state under mutex, then release before doing expensive work
743   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
744 
745   bool has_fixed_string = g_search_state.fixed_string && g_search_state.len > 0;
746   bool case_insensitive = g_search_state.case_insensitive;
747   char pattern_copy[LOG_SEARCH_INPUT_BUFFER_SIZE];
748   size_t pattern_len = g_search_state.len;
749   int pattern_count = g_search_state.active_pattern_count;
750   pcre2_singleton_t *patterns_copy[MAX_LOG_SEARCH_PATTERNS];
751 
752   if (has_fixed_string) {
753     // Parse the input buffer to extract just the pattern part (without flags)
754     grep_parse_result_t parsed = grep_parse_pattern(g_search_state.input_buffer);
755     const char *src = parsed.valid ? parsed.pattern : g_search_state.input_buffer;
756     SAFE_STRNCPY(pattern_copy, src, sizeof(pattern_copy) - 1);
757     pattern_copy[sizeof(pattern_copy) - 1] = '\0';
758     pattern_len = strlen(pattern_copy); // Update pattern_len for the actual parsed pattern
759   }
760 
761   for (int i = 0; i < pattern_count && i < MAX_LOG_SEARCH_PATTERNS; i++) {
762     patterns_copy[i] = g_search_state.active_patterns[i];
763   }
764 
765   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
766 
767   // Now do matching without holding mutex
768   size_t message_len = strlen(message);
769   bool matches = false;
770 
771   // Fixed string matching
772   if (has_fixed_string) {
773     const char *found = NULL;
774 
775     if (case_insensitive) {
776       found = utf8_strcasestr(message, pattern_copy);
777     } else {
778       found = strstr(message, pattern_copy);
779     }
780 
781     if (found) {
782       matches = true;
783 
784       // Convert byte position to character position (UTF-8 aware)
785       size_t byte_pos = (size_t)(found - message);
786       size_t char_pos = 0;
787       for (size_t i = 0; i < byte_pos && message[i] != '\0';) {
788         uint32_t codepoint;
789         int utf8_len = utf8_decode((const uint8_t *)(message + i), &codepoint);
790         if (utf8_len <= 0)
791           utf8_len = 1;
792         i += utf8_len;
793         char_pos++;
794       }
795 
796       // Count characters in the match (pattern_len is in bytes for the pattern)
797       size_t match_char_len = 0;
798       for (size_t i = 0; i < pattern_len && found[i] != '\0';) {
799         uint32_t codepoint;
800         int utf8_len = utf8_decode((const uint8_t *)(found + i), &codepoint);
801         if (utf8_len <= 0)
802           utf8_len = 1;
803         i += utf8_len;
804         match_char_len++;
805       }
806 
807       *out_match_start = char_pos;
808       *out_match_len = match_char_len;
809     }
810   }
811   // Regex pattern matching
812   else if (pattern_count > 0) {
813     pcre2_match_data *match_data = NULL;
814     for (int p = 0; p < pattern_count; p++) {
815       if (patterns_copy[p]) {
816         pcre2_code *code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(patterns_copy[p]);
817         if (code) {
818           match_data = pcre2_match_data_create_from_pattern(code, NULL);
819           break;
820         }
821       }
822     }
823 
824     if (match_data) {
825       for (int p = 0; p < pattern_count; p++) {
826         pcre2_singleton_t *singleton = patterns_copy[p];
827         if (!singleton) {
828           continue;
829         }
830 
831         pcre2_code *code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(singleton);
832         if (!code) {
833           continue;
834         }
835 
836         int rc = pcre2_jit_match(code, (PCRE2_SPTR)message, message_len, 0, 0, match_data, NULL);
837         if (rc >= 0) {
838           matches = true;
839           PCRE2_SIZE *ovector = pcre2_get_ovector_pointer(match_data);
840 
841           // Convert byte positions to character positions (UTF-8 aware)
842           size_t byte_start = (size_t)ovector[0];
843           size_t byte_end = (size_t)ovector[1];
844 
845           // Count characters up to byte_start
846           size_t char_start = 0;
847           for (size_t i = 0; i < byte_start && message[i] != '\0';) {
848             uint32_t codepoint;
849             int utf8_len = utf8_decode((const uint8_t *)(message + i), &codepoint);
850             if (utf8_len <= 0)
851               utf8_len = 1;
852             i += utf8_len;
853             char_start++;
854           }
855 
856           // Count characters up to byte_end
857           size_t char_end = char_start;
858           for (size_t i = byte_start; i < byte_end && message[i] != '\0';) {
859             uint32_t codepoint;
860             int utf8_len = utf8_decode((const uint8_t *)(message + i), &codepoint);
861             if (utf8_len <= 0)
862               utf8_len = 1;
863             i += utf8_len;
864             char_end++;
865           }
866 
867           *out_match_start = char_start;
868           *out_match_len = char_end - char_start;
869           break;
870         }
871       }
872       pcre2_match_data_free(match_data);
873     }
874   }
875 
876   return matches;
877 }






References asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(),
    atomic_load_int(), grep_parse_pattern(),
    LOG_SEARCH_INPUT_BUFFER_SIZE, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE,
    MAX_LOG_SEARCH_PATTERNS, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    grep_parse_result_t::pattern, pattern_count,
    SAFE_STRNCPY, utf8_decode(), utf8_strcasestr(), and
    grep_parse_result_t::valid.


Referenced by terminal_screen_render().






void
  * log_search_get_mutex (void )


Get the mutex protecting grep state.


Returns


Pointer to the grep state mutex (internal use only)


Used by terminal rendering to perform atomic read of grep input
    for consistent rendering without flicker.


Definition at line 935 of file search.c.


935                                  {
936   return (void *)&g_search_state.mutex;
937 }






Referenced by terminal_screen_render().






void
  * log_search_get_pattern_singleton (void )


Get the compiled regex pattern for interactive grep (internal
    use).


Returns


Opaque PCRE2 singleton for the current pattern, or NULL
  if inactive/no pattern


Internal function used by filter highlighting code to enable
    global match highlighting in interactive grep mode. Returns NULL when not in
    active mode or if pattern is fixed string type (no regex compilation).


Definition at line 910 of file search.c.


910                                              {
911   // Use atomic read to check if active
912   int mode = atomic_load_int(&g_search_state.mode_atomic);
913   if (mode == LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE) {
914     return NULL;
915   }
916 
917   // Safe read under mutex
918   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
919 
920   // Return the compiled pattern if we have one (not fixed string)
921   void *result = NULL;
922   if (g_search_state.active_pattern_count > 0 && !g_search_state.fixed_string) {
923     result = (void *)g_search_state.active_patterns[0];
924   }
925 
926   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
927 
928   return result;
929 }






References atomic_load_int(),
    LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE, mutex_lock, and
  mutex_unlock.


Referenced by grep_highlight_colored().






asciichat_error_t log_search_handle_key (keyboard_key_t
  key)


Process keyboard input for grep.


Parameters


key The keyboard key to process


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Uses keyboard_read_line_interactive() to handle text
    editing. Supports backspace, delete, arrows, home, end, enter, escape,
    UTF-8.




Pattern validation happens live via validator callback. Enter
    compiles and activates pattern. Escape cancels and restores previous
    patterns.


Definition at line 414 of file search.c.


414                                                             {
415   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
416 
417   // If not in input mode, check if user pressed '/'
418   if (g_search_state.mode != LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING) {
419     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
420 
421     // Check for '/' key (key already passed as parameter, don't read again!)
422     if (key == '/') {
423       log_search_enter_mode();
424     }
425     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
426   }
427 
428   // In input mode - use keyboard_read_line_interactive()
429   // CRITICAL: Keep mutex held during keyboard_read_line_interactive() to prevent races
430   // with the rendering thread. The validator callback and buffer modifications must be
431   // synchronized with readers in log_search_gather_and_filter_logs().
432   keyboard_line_edit_opts_t opts = {
433       .buffer = g_search_state.input_buffer,
434       .max_len = sizeof(g_search_state.input_buffer),
435       .len = &g_search_state.len,
436       .cursor = &g_search_state.cursor,
437       .echo = false,                       // We handle rendering ourselves
438       .mask_char = 0,                      // No masking
439       .prefix = NULL,                      // Don't render prefix - log_search_render_input_line() handles it
440       .validator = validate_pcre2_pattern, // Live validation
441       .key = key                           // Pass the pre-read key
442   };
443 
444   keyboard_line_edit_result_t result = keyboard_read_line_interactive(&opts);
445 
446   switch (result) {
447   case LINE_EDIT_ACCEPTED:
448     // Unlock before exit_mode since it acquires the mutex
449     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
450     log_search_exit_mode(true); // Parse and accept pattern
451     break;
452   case LINE_EDIT_CANCELLED:
453     // Unlock before exit_mode since it acquires the mutex
454     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
455     log_search_exit_mode(false); // Restore previous
456     break;
457   case LINE_EDIT_CONTINUE:
458     // Still editing - compile pattern for live filtering
459     // Mutex is still held here
460 
461     // If buffer is empty, clear patterns to show all logs (don't exit grep mode)
462     if (g_search_state.len == 0) {
463       g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 0;
464       mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
465       atomic_store_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender, true);
466       break;
467     }
468 
469     // Parse and compile pattern for live filtering
470     grep_parse_result_t parsed = grep_parse_pattern(g_search_state.input_buffer);
471 
472     if (!parsed.valid) {
473       // Invalid pattern - keep previous patterns active (don't clear)
474       mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
475       atomic_store_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender, true);
476       break;
477     }
478 
479     // Valid pattern - update filtering state
480     if (parsed.is_fixed_string) {
481       // Fixed string matching - no regex compilation needed
482       g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 0;
483       g_search_state.case_insensitive = parsed.case_insensitive;
484       g_search_state.fixed_string = true;
485     } else {
486       // Regex pattern - compile with full pcre2_options
487       pcre2_singleton_t *singleton = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(parsed.pattern, parsed.pcre2_options);
488       if (singleton) {
489         pcre2_code *code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(singleton);
490         if (code) {
491           g_search_state.active_patterns[0] = singleton;
492           g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 1;
493           g_search_state.case_insensitive = parsed.case_insensitive;
494           g_search_state.fixed_string = false;
495         } else {
496           // Compilation failed - fall back to fixed string matching
497           g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 0;
498           g_search_state.fixed_string = true;
499           g_search_state.case_insensitive = parsed.case_insensitive;
500         }
501       } else {
502         g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 0;
503         g_search_state.fixed_string = true;
504         g_search_state.case_insensitive = parsed.case_insensitive;
505       }
506     }
507 
508     // Store parsed flags
509     g_search_state.global_highlight = parsed.global_flag;
510     g_search_state.invert_match = parsed.invert;
511     g_search_state.context_before = parsed.context_before;
512     g_search_state.context_after = parsed.context_after;
513 
514     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
515     atomic_store_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender, true);
516     break;
517   case LINE_EDIT_NO_INPUT:
518     // No input available
519     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
520     break;
521   }
522 
523   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
524 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(),
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(), atomic_store_bool(),
    grep_parse_result_t::case_insensitive,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_after,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_before,
    grep_parse_result_t::global_flag, grep_parse_pattern(),
    grep_parse_result_t::invert,
    grep_parse_result_t::is_fixed_string,
    keyboard_read_line_interactive(), log_search_enter_mode(),
    log_search_exit_mode(), LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING,
    mutex_lock, mutex_unlock, grep_parse_result_t::pattern,
    grep_parse_result_t::pcre2_options, and
    grep_parse_result_t::valid.

    ui_status_display_interactive().






asciichat_error_t log_search_init (void )


Initialize interactive grep subsystem.


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Must be called before using any other log_search functions.
    Initializes state, mutex, and loads CLI --grep patterns if any.


Definition at line 125 of file search.c.


125                                         {
126   // Use lifecycle API for unified initialization
127   if (!lifecycle_init_once(&g_log_search_lifecycle)) {
128     // Already initialized, acquire mutex lock and return
129     mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
130     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
131   }
132 
133   // Winner of init_once - initialize mutex and state
134   g_log_search_lifecycle.sync_type = LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX;
135   g_log_search_lifecycle.sync.mutex = &g_search_state.mutex;
136   lifecycle_init(&g_log_search_lifecycle, "log_search");
137 
138   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
139 
140   // Initialize state (careful not to destroy the mutex!)
141   // Save the mutex before clearing state
142   mutex_t saved_mutex = g_search_state.mutex;
143   memset(&g_search_state, 0, sizeof(g_search_state));
144   // Restore the mutex
145   g_search_state.mutex = saved_mutex;
146 
147   // Check if there are CLI --grep patterns to use
148   const char *cli_pattern = grep_get_last_pattern();
149 
150   // Start in INACTIVE mode if no CLI pattern is provided.
151   // Only use patterns from CLI --grep arguments, not default patterns.
152   // This ensures logs are visible by default and only filtered if explicitly requested.
153   if (!cli_pattern || cli_pattern[0] == '\0') {
154     // No CLI pattern - start inactive
155     g_search_state.mode = LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE;
156     atomic_store_int(&g_search_state.mode_atomic, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE);
157     lifecycle_init_commit(&g_log_search_lifecycle);
158     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
159     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
160   }
161 
162   // Load CLI pattern into input buffer
163   SAFE_STRNCPY(g_search_state.input_buffer, cli_pattern, LOG_SEARCH_INPUT_BUFFER_SIZE - 1);
164   g_search_state.len = strlen(cli_pattern);
165   g_search_state.cursor = g_search_state.len;
166   g_search_state.mode = LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ACTIVE;
167   atomic_store_int(&g_search_state.mode_atomic, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ACTIVE);
168 
169   // Compile the initial pattern
170   grep_parse_result_t parsed = grep_parse_pattern(cli_pattern);
171   if (parsed.valid) {
172     pcre2_singleton_t *singleton = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(parsed.pattern, parsed.pcre2_options);
173     if (singleton) {
174       g_search_state.active_patterns[0] = singleton;
175       g_search_state.active_pattern_count = 1;
176     }
177     g_search_state.case_insensitive = parsed.case_insensitive;
178     g_search_state.fixed_string = parsed.is_fixed_string;
179     g_search_state.global_highlight = parsed.global_flag;
180     g_search_state.invert_match = parsed.invert;
181     g_search_state.context_before = parsed.context_before;
182     g_search_state.context_after = parsed.context_after;
183   }
184 
185   atomic_store_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender, true);
186   lifecycle_init_commit(&g_log_search_lifecycle);
187 
188   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
189   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
190 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(), atomic_store_bool(),
    atomic_store_int(), grep_parse_result_t::case_insensitive,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_after,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_before,
    grep_parse_result_t::global_flag, grep_get_last_pattern(),
    grep_parse_pattern(), grep_parse_result_t::invert,
    grep_parse_result_t::is_fixed_string, lifecycle_init(),
    lifecycle_init_commit(), lifecycle_init_once(),
    LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX, LOG_SEARCH_INPUT_BUFFER_SIZE,
    LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ACTIVE, LOG_SEARCH_MODE_INACTIVE,
    mutex_lock, mutex_unlock, grep_parse_result_t::pattern,
    grep_parse_result_t::pcre2_options, SAFE_STRNCPY, and
    grep_parse_result_t::valid.

Check if grep is in entering mode (async-signal-safe).


Returns


true if in GREP_MODE_ENTERING


Uses atomic load only (no mutex). Safe to call from signal
    handlers.

bool log_search_needs_rerender (void )


Check if screen needs immediate re-render.


Returns


true if pattern changed and re-render needed, false
  otherwise


Uses atomic flag for lock-free checking in render loops.
    Automatically clears flag after returning true.


Definition at line 883 of file search.c.


883                                      {
884   bool needs = atomic_load_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender);
885   if (needs) {
886     atomic_store_bool(&g_search_state.needs_rerender, false);
887   }
888   return needs;

void
  log_search_render_input_line (int width)


Render grep input line at bottom of screen.


Parameters


width Terminal width in columns


Renders: /<pattern>/flags█ with cursor position
    indicated. Cursor shown as block character or with background color.




If pattern is invalid (validator returns false), shows red
    background.


Definition at line 691 of file search.c.


691                                              {
692   (void)width; // Reserved for future use (line truncation)
693 
694   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
695 
696   if (g_search_state.mode != LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING) {
697     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
698     return;
699   }
700 
701   // Render the pattern and position terminal cursor at current edit position
702   char output_buf[512];
703 
704   // Build output: "/" + pattern
705   int pattern_len = snprintf(output_buf, sizeof(output_buf), "/%.*s", (int)g_search_state.len, g_search_state.input_buffer);
706 
707   if (pattern_len > 0 && pattern_len < (int)sizeof(output_buf)) {
708     // Write the pattern to terminal
709     platform_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, output_buf, pattern_len);
710 
711     // After writing, cursor is at: column = 1 (for "/") + len + 1
712     // We want it at: column = 1 (for "/") + cursor_position + 1
713     // So we need to move back by: len - cursor_position columns
714     int cursor_offset = (int)g_search_state.len - (int)g_search_state.cursor;
715     if (cursor_offset > 0) {
716       // Move left by the difference
717       terminal_move_cursor_relative(-cursor_offset);
718     }
719   }
720 
721   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
722 }






References LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING, mutex_lock,
    mutex_unlock, platform_write_all(), and
    terminal_move_cursor_relative().


Referenced by terminal_screen_render().






bool log_search_should_handle (int key)


Check if a key should be handled by grep module.


Parameters


key Keyboard key code


Returns


true if grep should handle this key, false to pass
  through


Returns true for:


    
  
  • 
  
  • All keys when in GREP_MODE_ENTERING (to edit pattern)
    • 



Definition at line 399 of file search.c.


399                                        {
400   mutex_lock(&g_search_state.mutex);
401 
402   // If in input mode, handle all keys
403   if (g_search_state.mode == LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING) {
404     mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
405     return true;
406   }
407 
408   // If not in input mode, only handle '/' to enter mode
409   bool should_handle = (key == '/');
410   mutex_unlock(&g_search_state.mutex);
411   return should_handle;
412 }






References LOG_SEARCH_MODE_ENTERING, mutex_lock, and
    mutex_unlock.

void
  log_search_signal_cancel (void )


Cancel grep mode from a signal handler (async-signal-safe). Sets
    an atomic flag that the status screen loop checks on its next iteration.
    Does not use mutexes or allocate memory.

    ascii-chat
